lesson.subtitle: Aula 14 -- Describing Trends -- Numbers Over Time
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Rafael de Andrade Brandão | Aula 14 -- Describing Trends

listenings.text: Hi Rafael, this is Diana again. Your quarterly presentation last week was excellent, and the board loved it. Now they want the bigger picture. For the next meeting, please describe the full-year trend, not just one quarter. Show how our sales moved over time, month by month. Start in January, when sales were low, and walk us through the year. Use a line chart. Explain when the numbers rose, when they fell, and when they stayed steady. Be specific: did sales rise sharply or slightly? When did we reach the peak? The board likes clear language, so use trend words: rose, fell, climbed, dropped, and remained steady. And remember to use the past simple, because the year already finished. Send me the line chart before Thursday. Thank you, and great work again.

Good morning, everyone. Today I am going to describe the sales trend for the full year. Let us look at the line chart. In January, sales were low, around one hundred thousand. Then, from February to April, sales rose sharply, by almost thirty percent. After that, in the summer, the numbers fell slightly, because of the holidays. In September, sales climbed again and reached the peak of the year in October. Finally, in November and December, the trend remained steady. Overall, it was a strong year. The line went up, then down a little, and then up to the peak. As you can see, the general trend was positive. That is the full-year picture. Thank you for listening. Are there any questions about the trend?

extra_audio.text: Diana asks Rafael to describe the full-year sales trend with a line chart. In January, sales were low. From February to April, sales rose sharply. In the summer, the numbers fell slightly. In October, sales reached the peak of the year. Finally, in November and December, the trend remained steady.
